problem.title: Research Problem
paragraphs: The core problem lies in the absence of a unified, intelligent, and adaptive smart museum system that can simultaneously address personalization, accessibility, navigation, and feedback. Existing solutions tend to focus on individual components rather than delivering a comprehensive platform that integrates these functionalities.

A large public museum environment where local and international visitors explore artifacts through traditional static displays and limited digital interaction. Primary users include museum visitors, students and educational groups, while secondary users encompass museum staff, tour guides, and cultural heritage researchers.

Therefore, this research aims to address the problem of designing and implementing an AI-driven, integrated smart museum guide system that combines QR-based artifact access, role-based personalized content, multilingual support, intelligent recommendations, interactive 2D/3D visualization, adaptive indoor navigation, and emotion-aware feedback to enhance visitor experience and support data-driven museum management.

solution.title: Proposed Solution
paragraphs: This research proposes an AI-driven Smart Museum Guide system for the Colombo National Museum that unifies multiple technologies into a single, cohesive platform designed to transform the visitor experience.

The system integrates QR-based artifact access with role-based personalization, AI-powered 2D reconstruction and 3D visualization, adaptive indoor navigation using BLE beacon positioning, and an emotion-based feedback system supported by a multilingual conversational chatbot.

By combining all components into a unified mobile application and backend architecture, the system ensures seamless interaction, scalability, and real-time data processing — enabling smart museum operations and data-driven management decisions.
